title: syringa amurensis var. pekinensis
intro: Syringa amurensis var. pekinensis, also known as the Amur lilac, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to northeastern Asia. The medicinal properties of Syringa amurensis var. pekinensis lie in its flowers, leaves, and roots, which have been used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health. Syringa amurensis var. pekinensis can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
